patch.value: GH-578: pm context summary.blocked and pm list-blocked count status-only, disagreeing with edge-aware pm next

GH-489/pm-l0bu made pm next edge-aware (items with open blocked_by deps count as blocked), but the two other read surfaces agents use still count ONLY status:"blocked": pm context (ctx) summary.blocked reports 0 at every depth, and pm list-blocked reports count:0, while pm next's blocked companion lists the same items with open_blocker_count. Since auto-unblock (GH-267) makes status=blocked rare, these counters are effectively dead fields — an orchestrator reading blocked:0 concludes everything is dispatchable while pm next disagrees. Fix = adopt ONE shared blocked-semantics helper (the pm next edge-aware definition) across context summary + list-blocked, or report both dimensions explicitly (blocked_status + blocked_by_dependencies) so the surfaces can never silently disagree. Fix in the shared status-derivation layer, not per-surface — pm-pfqi (list-* consolidation) and pm-kcs4 (context-noun consolidation) will inherit whichever semantics ship here, so land the shared helper first or with them.

pm context summary.blocked counts dependency-blocked items identically to pm next's blocked companion at every depth; pm list-blocked returns the same set; a parity spec asserts next/context/list-blocked agree on a workspace with edge-blocked (status open) and status-blocked items; docs state the single definition of blocked

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Code evidence (2026-07-17 pass #95): context summary blocked counter at src/cli/commands/context.ts:1126-1132 increments only via isBlockedStatus(desc.status, statusRegistry) — status-only, no dependency-edge awareness; agenda ranking helpers at :675-682/:717 are likewise status-registry-driven. list-blocked is a status-filter list variant registered at src/cli/register-list-query.ts:656 (one of the 8 variants pm-pfqi consolidates). pm next's edge-aware definition (open_blocker_count from blocked_by deps, shipped for GH-489/pm-l0bu in PR#502) is the semantics to adopt. pm stats reports generic by-status buckets (honest labeling, NOT in scope). Fix belongs in a shared blocked-semantics helper consumed by next + context + list-blocked so pm-pfqi/pm-kcs4 consolidations inherit it.
